Built directly from the valuation PDF. Normal retirement rates are published by age and sex; early retirement rates are published by age. The current TRS-style runtime accepts one normal schedule, so the build uses the arithmetic average of male and female normal rates by age.
notes: Source-backed first cut. Ages below published retirement ages are zero-filled; 65-69 and 70-74 are expanded to single years; 75+ is carried through the txtrs-av max_age horizon.

page_reference_notes: Built directly from the valuation PDF preserving the published select-and-ultimate structure. Service years 1-10 are mapped to runtime lookup values 0-9; post-select rates are stored by years_from_nr with an explicit runtime row for 0 years from normal retirement at rate 0.
notes: Source-backed first cut. Runtime keeps the published years-from-normal-retirement structure rather than pre-expanding by tier.

page_reference_notes: Built directly from the valuation PDF. Table 2 supplies projected payroll, AAL, AVA, and admin-expense rate. Table 4 supplies market value of assets and the remaining AVA deferral after the 2024 valuation.
table_or_section_labels: Table 2 Summary of Cost Items; Table 4 Development of Actuarial Value of Assets
notes: Source-strong first-cut funding seed. All opening values are valuation-derived. The only runtime-specific transform is mapping the remaining 2023 AVA deferral into defer_y2_legacy because the model stores gain/loss smoothing in bucket form rather than as the published aggregate remaining balance.

page_reference_notes: Table 18 is on printed page 42 / PDF 49. Built directly from the valuation PDF by lumping the five pre-retirement bands (Up to 35 through 55-59) into runtime ages 55-59, spreading each later 5-year band evenly across its ages, and spreading the published 100 & up band evenly across runtime ages 100-120.
table_or_section_labels: Table 18 Distribution of Life Annuities by Age
notes: Source-faithful AV transform. Scope is life annuities only (475,891 retirees). Disabled annuities (Table 19, 12,030) and survivor annuities (18,291) excluded from this first cut as a documented modeling simplification — see issue #71.

page_reference_notes: AV mortality narrative is on printed pp. 60 (active basis) and 64 (retiree basis) / PDF pp. 67 and 71. Active employee qx is source-direct from SOA Pub-2010 Amount-Weighted Below-Median-Income Teacher (PubT-2010(B)) Employee column for ages 18-80; ages 81-120 are filled from the retiree column per the runtime auto-fill rule. Healthy retiree qx is from the txtrs-av prototype TX-custom 2021 estimator backprojected to 2010 using UMP-2021 ultimate rates so the runtime (which assumes base_year=2010) reprojects forward to the prototype's published-checkpoint-anchored 2021 fit.
table_or_section_labels: PubT-2010(B) Teachers Below-Median Income; AV mortality assumptions narrative; estimated_retiree_mortality_base_2021
notes: Active half is source-direct transcription. Retiree half is the documented fallback estimator mortality-checkpoint-spline-estimation-v1 because the AV-named 2021 TRS of Texas Healthy Pensioner Mortality Tables are not publicly available. AV's male set-forward 2 years for actives is applied at runtime via plan_config.modeling.male_mp_forward_shift, not in this file. See issue #71 for disabled-retiree exclusion. See issue #72 for retiree-table fallback status and tail-age fit. See issue #73 for active-improvement-scale choice.

page_reference_notes: AV cites Scale UMP 2021 with immediate convergence on printed p. 64 / PDF 71. Implemented as the SOA MP-2021 ultimate column (2037+) replicated flat across years 1951-2037 by age and gender. Ultimate rates are unisex by age in MP-2021.
table_or_section_labels: Scale UMP 2021 (ultimate rates of MP-2021); AV mortality assumptions narrative
notes: Source-direct transcription of MP-2021 ultimate column. Immediate-convergence interpretation is inferred from AV plus experience study plus GASB 67; documented as such, not directly published. See issue #73.

table_or_section_labels: Long-term return rate is the AV-stated 7.00% investment return assumption (Table 12c HISTORY OF INVESTMENT RETURNS). Recession scenarios are modeling-choice stress paths mirrored from the legacy txtrs convention: one near-term recession at start_year, recovery over three years, second recession in recur_recession 15 years later.
source_units: AV Table 12c assumption rate; legacy txtrs return scenario shape
column19: Modeling-choice artifact, not document-sourced. The runtime overrides the model and assumption columns with plan_config economic.model_return and economic.dr_current at runtime, so those columns are nominal placeholders. Recession, recur_recession, and constant_6 are stress scenarios used only with --scenario flags.

page_reference_notes: Derived from pvfb_term_current (calibration.json) plus economic.dr_current and benefit.cola.current_retire and term_vested.avg_deferral_years and term_vested.avg_payout_years (plan_config.json). No external document source.
notes: Documented estimation: deferred-then-level-with-COLA stream calibrated so NPV at dr_current equals pvfb_term_current. Per-plan parameters D=12 deferral years and L=25 payout years are first-cut defaults to be refined against valuation term-vested demographics. Built by scripts/build/build_av_term_vested_cashflow.py txtrs-av and verified by scripts/build/verify_term_vested_cashflow.py. Method spec: prep/common/methods/term_vested_deferred_annuity.md. Plan-specific parameter notes: prep/txtrs-av/methods/term_vested_deferred_annuity.md. See issue #76.
